invalid_review_attempts.reason: full source prompt-argument invocation was terminated after prolonged 0-byte output and produced no review payload

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-177 can close production_ready. This is a synthetic, no-write Matter Core foundation/descriptor pack (contract + registry + immutable model descriptors + validators + tests + H05/C05 packets), and the "production_ready" flag (matter_core_foundation_wiki_graph_skeleton_verified) denotes a verified foundation artifact, not shipped runtime — consistent with the no-write attestation and the prior CP00-172..176 closeouts.

Q1 (no early RP functionality): SATISFIED. Attestation flags implements_graph_provider_runtime / implements_citation_ledger_runtime / implements_loop_engine / exposes_client_visible_wiki_output are all false, and the foundation validator asserts graph runtime unselected, CitationLedger reserved-reference-only (CP00-198/RP06), and Loop stable-reference-only (CP00-552/RP18). MatterGraph is descriptor_only vocabulary (18 node / 14 edge types); MatterWiki is a shell. No runtime is moved early.

Q2 (Wiki/Graph source/permission/audit/review/no-write boundaries): SUFFICIENT for closeout. Wiki sections default under_review + uncited_internal_note; client-visible output requires permission trimming + human review and is blocked by a validator; source-link policy enforced. Graph requires deterministic upsert/query/permission_trimmed_traversal/snapshot/audit metadata; cross-matter SIMILAR_TO is disabled unless policy allows with similarity/permission metadata validated. No-write attestation (synthetic_only=true, no_real_client_data=true, all state/runtime flags false) is comprehensive.

Q3 (tests/validators adequate): YES for a descriptor/foundation pack. Each key safety invariant has a corresponding test (plan binding, vocabulary, no-write shell/skeleton, missing-source-link + unreviewed-client-visible blocks, SIMILAR_TO metadata, v2/Loop boundary preservation), backed by coverage/foundation/registry/packet/handoff validators and the standalone validator script. Counts reconcile (deliverables sum 150; phases 52+98=150; 11 models). Full suite green: npm test 1248/1248, node --test 6/6, all rp05/closeout/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal validators, build, node --check, git diff --check.

Q4 (blockers): None. No unresolved P0/P1/P2. Three P3 advisories below are forward-looking/non-blocking and do not gate closeout.

Review basis note: direct source inspection tools were unavailable in this context, so this assessment relies on the structured change summary plus the validation evidence. For a no-write descriptor pack the validator/test suite is the controlling verification, and it passes green — including the standalone validator that asserts the no-write/no-runtime/no-loop flags and the foundation boundaries that would catch divergence between stated facts and code.

findings.title: Test granularity is integration-level (6 cases) vs 11 models / 9 planned test units
findings.details: The executable suite verifies 6 integration-style cases covering 11 model descriptors and multiple validator branches, while the pack plan budgets 9 test deliverable units. Each stated safety invariant is covered, but per-model immutability/field-shape assertions and per-validator-branch negative cases are bundled rather than enumerated, which weakens regression localization if a single descriptor or validator branch later regresses. Non-blocking for a no-write descriptor pack since the foundation/coverage validators and the standalone validator script provide additional structural enforcement.
findings.recommendation: In a follow-on pack, add explicit per-model no-write/immutability and field-shape assertions, plus per-branch negative tests for each validator gate (source-link, client-visible review, SIMILAR_TO policy), to tighten regression coverage and traceability to the 9 planned test units.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory; non-blocking — accepted for CP00-177 closeout, recommend addressing in a future RP05 pack.

findings.title: Graph vocabulary frozen as descriptors now; guard against drift when provider runtime lands
findings.details: The 18 node types and 14 edge types are frozen as descriptor-only vocabulary while the graph provider runtime is deferred (provider_runtime_selected=false). When the runtime/Neo4j traversal pack is implemented in a future RP, the eventual provider schema could drift from this CP177 frozen vocabulary with no automated binding between them. This is a forward-looking concern only; the current descriptor set is internally consistent and validated.
findings.recommendation: When the graph provider runtime pack is implemented, include a conformance test asserting the runtime schema (node/edge types) exactly matches the CP177 frozen vocabulary so descriptor and runtime cannot silently diverge.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory; non-blocking — forward-looking guardrail for the future graph-runtime pack.

findings.title: Cross-matter SIMILAR_TO is the highest confidentiality risk; enforce policy gate server-side when traversal runtime is built
findings.details: SIMILAR_TO drives cross-matter similarity, which in a law-firm context implicates information-barrier / ethical-wall confidentiality between client matters. CP177 correctly gates it as disabled-unless-policy-allows with similarity/permission metadata validated at the descriptor layer, which is adequate for this no-write pack. The residual risk is that descriptor-level metadata is not the same as enforcement at query time once permission_trimmed_traversal becomes executable.
findings.recommendation: When permission_trimmed_traversal runtime lands, enforce the cross-matter SIMILAR_TO policy gate server-side at query time (not via descriptor metadata alone) and add an explicit default-deny test proving cross-matter similarity traversal is blocked unless policy explicitly permits it.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory; non-blocking — current descriptor gating is sufficient for closeout; carry into the future traversal-runtime pack.
